StrengthMultiplier: Multiplies the HP of the Lord. Standard = 1, Rat = 0.5, Wolf = 1.5
DotColour: The color of the dots indicating the lords strength. Vanilla uses yellow for weak AIs and blue for strong ones
DotCount: The amount of dots indicating the lords strength. Vanilla uses one dot per 0.1 of multiplier
Type: The type of lord that spawns on the keep. Either Arab (Saladin) or Europ (Richard)
ArabArcher: Amount of ArabArchers that spawn by the keep at game start
Slave: Amount of Slaves that spawn by the keep at game start
Slinger: Amount of Slingers that spawn by the keep at game start
Assassin: Amount of Assassins that spawn by the keep at game start
HorseArcher: Amount of HorseArchers that spawn by the keep at game start
ArabSwordsman: Amount of ArabSwordsmans that spawn by the keep at game start
FireThrower: Amount of FireThrowers that spawn by the keep at game start
EuropArcher: Amount of EuropArchers that spawn by the keep at game start
Spearman: Amount of Spearmans that spawn by the keep at game start
Maceman: Amount of Macemans that spawn by the keep at game start
Crossbowman: Amount of Crossbowmans that spawn by the keep at game start
Pikeman: Amount of Pikemans that spawn by the keep at game start
Swordsman: Amount of Swordsmans that spawn by the keep at game start
Knigth: Amount of Knigths that spawn by the keep at game start
Monk: Amount of Monks that spawn by the keep at game start
WallDecoration: The type of decoration the lord uses when placing flg in the aiv-editor. 8 = oil pouring out with fire, 9 = burning flames, 10 = small flag, 11 = big flag, 12 = medium flag, 13 = crusader flag, 14 = braziers, 15 = decorative skulls, 21 = steam clouds, 22 = disease clouds, 26 = fire explosions, 28 = ravens flying down, 29 = medium rocks landing in water, 30 = small rocks hitting structures, 31 = small rocks landing in water, 40 = zero gold rising note, 41 = zero popularity rising note, 42 = blank rising note, 43 = blank rising note
CriticalPopularity: Below this popularity, the AI sells goods aggressively to raise gold. 10000 = 100 popularity.
LowestPopularity: Below this popularity, AI sets taxes towards TaxesMin and gives extra rations until HighestPopularity is reached. 10000 = 100 popularity.
HighestPopularity: Above this popularity, AI increases taxes until a stable popularity modifier of up to -3 is achieved. 10000 = 100 popularity.
Farm1: type of the 1. Farm that the AI builds.
Farm2: type of the 2. Farm that the AI builds.
Farm3: type of the 3. Farm that the AI builds.
Farm4: type of the 4. Farm that the AI builds.
Farm5: type of the 5. Farm that the AI builds.
Farm6: type of the 6. Farm that the AI builds.
Farm7: type of the 7. Farm that the AI builds.
Farm8: type of the 8. Farm that the AI builds. After the 8. farm the AI will start over from the top. Destroyed farms will not be replaced by the same type of farm, but with the next in the queue.
PopulationPerFarm: For every this amount of people the AI can house it will build one farm. The first farm is always build at 0 population. Hop farms dont count towards this value and are built whenever next in queue
PopulationPerWoodcutter: For every this amount of people the AI can house it will build one woodcutter. When the AI reaches this amount for the first time two are build instead.
PopulationPerQuarry: For every this amount of people the AI can house it will build one Quarry. When the AI reaches this amount for the first time two are build instead.
PopulationPerIronmine: For every this amount of people the AI can house it will build one Ironmine. When the AI reaches this amount for the first time two are build instead.
PopulationPerPitchrig: For every this amount of people the AI can house it will build one Pitchrig.
MaxQuarries: Maximum amount of Quarrys the AI will build
MaxIronmines: Maximum amount of Ironmines the AI will build
MaxWoodcutters: Maximum amount of Woodcutters the AI will build
MaxPitchrigs: Maximum amount of Pitchrigs the AI will build
MaxFarms: Maximum amount of Farms the AI will build. Hop Farms dont count towards this value.
BuildInterval: The speed at which the AI builds. Counts for everything, AIV buildings, non-AIV buildings and all kinds of repairs. 1 is the fastest.
ResourceRebuildDelay: The time the AI waits before trying to rebuild a non-AIV recource building. When rebuilding a Farm the AI builds the next one in the queue until it has build a different Farm then a Hop Farm.
MaxFood: The maximum amount stored of Apples, Cheese, Meat, Bread, Wheat and Hop (+ResourceVariance on top). Amount choosen is per type, NOT combined.
MinimumApples: If the amount of apples stored falls below this value, the AI will buy [TradeAmountFood] apples, until reaching this value again. If = 0, the AI will never buy apples. If = -1 the AI will sell all apples.
MinimumCheese: If the amount of cheese stored falls below this value, the AI will buy [TradeAmountFood] cheese, until reaching this value again. If = 0, the AI will never buy cheese. If = -1 the AI will sell all cheese.
MinimumBread: If the amount of bread stored falls below this value, the AI will buy [TradeAmountFood] bread, until reaching this value again. If = 0, the AI will never buy bread. If = -1 the AI will sell all bread. Can be better to leave at 0 and buy wheat instead with bread-eco
MinimumWheat: If the amount of wheat stored falls below this value, the AI will buy [TradeAmountFood] wheat, until reaching this value again. If = 0, the AI will never buy wheat. If = -1 the AI will sell all wheat
MinimumHop: If the amount of hop stored falls below this value, the AI will buy [TradeAmountFood] hop, until reaching this value again. If = 0, the AI will never buy hop. If = -1 the AI will sell all hop.
TradeAmountFood: The amount of food the AI buys at a time...
TradeAmountEquipment: The amount of Weapons the AI buys at a time...
AIRequestDelay: The frequency with which the AI asks the player for resources (lower value -> more frequent).
MinimumGoodsRequiredAfterTrade: On recource request of a player...
DoubleRationsFoodThreshold: If the combined amount of Apples, Cheese, Meat and Bread is bigger than this value, the AI will give double rations.
MaxWood: The maximum amount stored of Wood (+ResourceVariance on top).
MaxStone: The maximum amount stored of Stone (+ResourceVariance on top).
MaxResourceOther: The maximum amount stored of Iron, Pitch and Flour (+ResourceVariance on top).
MaxEquipment: The maximum amount stored of Weapons and Armors (+ResourceVariance on top).
MaxBeer: The maximum amount stored of Beer (+ResourceVariance on top).
MaxResourceVariance: The deviation allowed from any 'Max[Recource]' Value.
RecruitGoldThreshold: The minimum amount of gold the AI needs to have, before it starts recruiting units.
TargetChoice: Sets the way the AI chooses the target of an attack.
RunningUnits_Macemen: If true, maceman will always run...
RunningUnits_Spearmen: If true, spearman will always run...
RunningUnits_Slingers: If true, slingers will always run...
RunningUnits_Slaves: If true, slaves will always run...
